# pi-gateway — full reference

This document is a complete, self-contained reference for AI consumption. It mirrors `CLAUDE.md` plus the README, with every endpoint, every schema rule, every security default, every config knob, and every architectural pattern.

---

## Identity

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Name | `pi-gateway` |
| Type | OpenAI-compatible HTTP frontend on top of `pi.dev` |
| Shape | LiteLLM-style stateless protocol translator |
| License | MIT |
| npm | https://www.npmjs.com/package/pi-gateway |
| Source | https://github.com/bntvllnt/pi-gateway |
| Author | https://github.com/bntvllnt |

## Goals

1. Re-expose every model `pi-coding-agent`'s `ModelRegistry` can reach (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Mistral, Groq, Cerebras, xAI, OpenRouter, Bedrock, Vertex, Azure OpenAI Responses, ZAI, MiniMax, Hugging Face, plus OAuth subscriptions: Claude Pro/Max, ChatGPT Plus/Pro Codex, GitHub Copilot, Gemini CLI, Antigravity) via the canonical OpenAI Chat Completions wire format.
2. Work from any client that supports a custom OpenAI base URL: Open WebUI, LibreChat, LiteLLM consumers, Cursor (Override OpenAI Base URL), Continue.dev, Cline, Aider, ChatGPT-Next-Web, BetterChatGPT, Lobe Chat.
3. Reuse pi's auth (`~/.pi/agent/auth.json` resolved via `AuthStorage` + `ModelRegistry`) so users authenticate once.
4. Run two ways from one repo: **standalone CLI** (`pi-gateway --port 4000`) and **pi-extension** (slash commands inside `pi`).

## Non-goals (do NOT add)

1. No pi `AgentSession` is created at any point.
2. No system prompt, tools, or skills are injected by pi-gateway.
3. No tool *execution* — `tools`/`tool_choice` are forwarded; the client executes and posts `role: "tool"` results.
4. No conversation state — each `POST /v1/chat/completions` is independent.
5. No in-process TLS — front with Caddy / Tailscale Funnel / ngrok.
6. No `--api-key` CLI flag — argv leaks via `/proc/<pid>/cmdline` + `ps aux`.

## Install

### Standalone

```bash
pnpm dlx pi-gateway --port 4000
# or
npm i -g pi-gateway && pi-gateway --port 4000
```

### Pi extension (auto-installs into pi runtime)

Add to `~/.pi/agent/settings.json`:

```jsonc
{
  "packages": [
    // ...
    "git:git@github.com:bntvllnt/pi-gateway"
  ]
}
```

Restart `pi`. The extension auto-clones to `~/.pi/agent/git/github.com/bntvllnt/pi-gateway/` and registers slash commands, LLM-callable tools, and a footer widget.

Inside pi:

```
/gateway:start    # spawn the daemon (detached, survives pi exit)
/gateway:status   # show url + pid + model count
/gateway:stop     # SIGTERM
```

## CLI flags (standalone)

| Flag | Default | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| `--port N` | `4000` | `0` = OS-assigned |
| `--bind HOST` | `127.0.0.1` | Non-loopback requires `apiKey` in config file (NOT a CLI flag) |
| `--config PATH` | — | Extra JSON layered after `~/.pi/agent/gateway.json` |
| `--auth-dir PATH` | `~/.pi/agent` | Where to find `auth.json` and `models.json` |
| `--allow-origin ORIGIN` | empty | Repeatable; `"*"` = any |
| `--log-level LEVEL` | `info` | `debug` / `info` / `warn` / `error` |
| `--model-allowlist ID` | — | Repeatable; matches `provider/model-id` or bare id |
| `--model-denylist ID` | — | Repeatable |
| `--expose-oauth-subscriptions` | loopback default-on | Expose OAuth subscriptions on non-loopback too |
| `--no-expose-oauth-subscriptions` | — | Hide OAuth subscriptions even on loopback |
| `--require-key-on-loopback` | off | Force bearer auth on `127.0.0.1` |
| `--version` | — | Print version + exit |
| `--help` | — | Show help |

Subcommands:

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `pi-gateway` | Run the daemon foreground |
| `pi-gateway models` | Print available models then exit |

## Config file: `~/.pi/agent/gateway.json`

```jsonc
{
  "port": 4000,
  "bindAddress": "127.0.0.1",
  "apiKey": "long-random-string",        // ONLY place a key can live; argv is refused
  "allowedOrigins": ["http://localhost:5173"],
  "requireKeyOnLoopback": false,
  "exposeOAuthSubscriptions": true,      // on loopback this is the safe default
  "modelAllowlist": null,                 // null = expose all
  "modelDenylist": null,
  "logLevel": "info",
  "heartbeatIntervalMs": 15000,
  "forceAbortTimeoutMs": 5000
}
```

Resolution precedence (high → low): **CLI flags > `PI_GATEWAY_*` env vars > `--config PATH` > `~/.pi/agent/gateway.json` > built-in defaults**.

## Env vars

| Var | Purpose |
|-----|---------|
| `PI_GATEWAY_PORT` | Port |
| `PI_GATEWAY_BIND` | Bind address |
| `PI_GATEWAY_API_KEY` | Bearer token (allowed via env; **not** via CLI flag) |
| `PI_GATEWAY_AUTH_DIR` | Auth dir |
| `PI_GATEWAY_LOG_LEVEL` | Log level |
| `PI_GATEWAY_EXPOSE_OAUTH` | `true`/`false` |

## Endpoints

### `GET /healthz`

```json
{ "ok": true, "uptimeMs": 1234 }
```

### `GET /v1/models`

```json
{
  "object": "list",
  "data": [
    { "id": "openai-codex/gpt-5.4", "object": "model", "created": 1779893681, "owned_by": "openai-codex" },
    ...
  ]
}
```

Rules:

- `id` is always `provider/model-id` (Open WebUI / LiteLLM / OpenRouter convention).
- Sort is stable (alphabetic by `id`).
- OAuth subscriptions default-allow on loopback; default-deny on non-loopback unless `--expose-oauth-subscriptions` or `exposeOAuthSubscriptions: true`.
- `created` is a stable per-process timestamp.
- `allowlist`/`denylist` are applied.

### `POST /v1/chat/completions`

#### Request (subset of OpenAI Chat Completions)

Accepted fields: `model` (required), `messages` (required), `stream`, `stream_options`, `temperature`, `top_p`, `max_tokens`, `max_completion_tokens`, `stop`, `presence_penalty`, `frequency_penalty`, `response_format`, `seed`, `user`, `tools`, `tool_choice`, `n` (must be 1).

`messages[].role`: `system` | `developer` | `user` | `assistant` | `tool` (Chat Completions shape).

#### Non-stream response

```json
{
  "id": "chatcmpl-<hex>",
  "object": "chat.completion",
  "created": 1779894353,
  "model": "openai-codex/gpt-5.4",
  "system_fingerprint": "fp_pi_<hex>",
  "choices": [
    {
      "index": 0,
      "message": {
        "role": "assistant",
        "content": "...",
        "reasoning_content": "..."  // optional, only for reasoning models
      },
      "finish_reason": "stop",
      "logprobs": null
    }
  ],
  "usage": {
    "prompt_tokens": 27,
    "completion_tokens": 10,
    "total_tokens": 37
  }
}
```

#### Stream chunks (`Content-Type: text/event-stream`)

Headers:

```
Content-Type:        text/event-stream; charset=utf-8
Cache-Control:       no-cache, no-transform
Connection:          keep-alive
X-Accel-Buffering:   no
```

Frames:

```
data: {"id":"chatcmpl-...","object":"chat.completion.chunk","created":...,"model":"...","system_fingerprint":"...","choices":[{"index":0,"delta":{"role":"assistant"},"finish_reason":null,"logprobs":null}]}

data: {"id":"chatcmpl-...","object":"chat.completion.chunk","choices":[{"index":0,"delta":{"content":"Hello"},"finish_reason":null,"logprobs":null}]}

...

data: {"id":"chatcmpl-...","object":"chat.completion.chunk","choices":[{"index":0,"delta":{},"finish_reason":"stop","logprobs":null}],"usage":{"prompt_tokens":...,"completion_tokens":...,"total_tokens":...}}

data: [DONE]

```

Invariants:

- Single `chatcmpl-<id>` reused across **every** chunk and matches the non-stream `id` shape.
- First chunk's delta has `role: "assistant"`.
- Subsequent text chunks have `delta.content` (string).
- Thinking content (Claude 3.7+, codex reasoning) → `delta.reasoning_content` (OpenRouter/Ollama convention). **Never** in non-stream `content` (which is a string).
- Tool calls → `delta.tool_calls[].function.arguments` is a **JSON string** (NEVER an object).
- Final chunk carries `finish_reason` + (if `stream_options.include_usage`) the usage block.
- Terminator: `data: [DONE]\n\n`.
- Heartbeat: `: heartbeat\n\n` every 15s during silent periods (Claude extended thinking).

#### Mid-stream errors

OpenAI convention: emit one error frame then close. **No trailing `[DONE]`** (would signal normal completion).

```
data: {"error":{"type":"upstream_error","code":"provider_error","message":"..."}}

(connection close)
```

Status codes:

| HTTP | When |
|------|------|
| `200` | Successful Chat Completions |
| `400` | Invalid JSON, schema mismatch, image part on non-image model, unsupported `response_format` |
| `401` | Missing/invalid bearer (non-loopback or `requireKeyOnLoopback: true`); no credentials for the resolved provider |
| `403` | OAuth-subscription model requested on non-loopback without `--expose-oauth-subscriptions` |
| `404` | Unknown model id; unknown route |
| `499` | Client disconnected (non-stream) |
| `502` | Upstream provider errored (pi-ai returned `stopReason: "error"`) |

Error envelope:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "type": "<error type>",
    "code": "<machine code>",
    "message": "<human message>",
    "param": "<optional field name>"
  }
}
```

## Stop-reason mapping

| pi-ai `stopReason` | OpenAI `finish_reason` | HTTP status |
|-------------------|------------------------|-------------|
| `stop` | `stop` | 200 |
| `length` | `length` | 200 |
| `toolUse` | `tool_calls` | 200 |
| `error` (non-stream) | — | **502** with error envelope |
| `error` (stream, after deltas) | — | mid-stream error frame, no `[DONE]` |
| `aborted` (non-stream) | — | **499** |
| `aborted` (stream) | — | abort signal propagates, stream ends |

## Usage block mapping

| pi-ai `Usage` field | OpenAI `usage` field |
|---------------------|----------------------|
| `input` | `prompt_tokens` |
| `output` | `completion_tokens` |
| `input + output` | `total_tokens` |

## Multi-block content rules

Claude 3.7+ assistant messages contain both `thinking` and `text` content blocks. OpenAI `choices[].message.content` is a string. pi-gateway merges as follows:

- Thinking blocks: **dropped** from non-stream `content`; surfaced **only** during streaming as `delta.reasoning_content`.
- Text blocks: concatenated with `\n\n` if multiple.

## Tool-calling translation

Outbound (pi-ai → OpenAI):

- pi-ai `ToolCall.arguments` (object) → `function.arguments` (JSON string via `JSON.stringify`)
- pi-ai `id` → OpenAI `id`
- pi-ai `name` → OpenAI `function.name`

Inbound (OpenAI → pi-ai), for follow-up `role: "tool"` messages:

- OpenAI carries only `tool_call_id`; pi-ai's `ToolResultMessage` requires `toolName`.
- Recovered by scanning the messages array for the preceding `assistant.tool_calls[].id → function.name`.
- Stateless per-request lookup; never session state.

## Model id resolution

Accepted forms:

| Form | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| `provider/model-id` | Exact lookup via `ModelRegistry.find(provider, modelId)` |
| `model-id` | First available match across all providers (with disambiguation by load order) |
| `provider/model-id:thinking` | Treated as `provider/model-id` (thinking level handled by pi-ai) |

Unknown id → HTTP 404 `model_not_found`.

## Security defaults

- **Default bind**: `127.0.0.1`. Implementation asserts `server.address().address` matches resolved host after `listening` event (Node's `listen(port)` default is `0.0.0.0`).
- **Non-loopback** bind requires `apiKey` in config file; refused if `apiKey` is missing.
- **`--api-key` CLI flag** is refused (argv leak). Use config file or `PI_GATEWAY_API_KEY` env var.
- **PID lockfile** at `~/.pi/agent/gateway.pid`, atomic `fs.openSync(path, "wx")` (`O_CREAT|O_EXCL`). On EEXIST probes liveness with `process.kill(pid, 0)`; stale → unlink + retry once.
- **SIGINT/SIGTERM** handlers registered BEFORE `listen()`. Abort in-flight pi-ai streams via `AbortSignal`. Allow up to `forceAbortTimeoutMs` (5s default) for sockets to flush.
- **Log redaction**: hardcoded allowlist for safe headers (`content-type`, `content-length`, `user-agent`, `accept`, `accept-encoding`, `host`). Everything else (including `authorization`, anything matching `*token*` / `*key*` / `*secret*`) is omitted.
- **CORS**: empty `allowedOrigins` by default = no Access-Control-Allow-Origin header. Add origins explicitly (`"*"` allowed).
- **OAuth subscriptions**: default-allow on loopback; default-deny on non-loopback unless `--expose-oauth-subscriptions`.

## Source-of-truth schema

`schemas/openresponses.openapi.json` — pinned copy of https://www.openresponses.org/openapi/openapi.json (OpenAPI 3.1.0, "OpenAI API" v2.3.0). 108 component schemas. Defines `POST /responses` + `POST /responses/compact` (Responses API).

pi-gateway v1 implements **Chat Completions**. Chat Completions schemas are inlined in `tests/contract.mjs` from the published OpenAI reference. When pi-gateway adds `/v1/responses` (v1.1+), validation against the pinned doc covers the full request/response cycle.

To refresh the pinned doc:

```js
node -e "(async () => { const r = await fetch('https://www.openresponses.org/openapi/openapi.json'); require('fs').writeFileSync('schemas/openresponses.openapi.json', await r.text()); })()"
```

Commit the diff. The contract test fails CI if schemas drift from what we serve.

## File layout

```
.
├── index.ts                  Pi-extension entry (slash commands, footer, tools, detached-child spawn)
├── src/
│   ├── cli.ts                Standalone CLI entry → dist/cli.js
│   ├── config.ts             Layered config
│   ├── lifecycle.ts          State machine + atomic PID lockfile + signal wiring
│   ├── protocol/
│   │   ├── chat-completions.ts   Typebox request schema
│   │   └── models-list.ts        /v1/models payload + OAuth-subscription filter
│   ├── translate/
│   │   ├── openai-to-pi.ts       Request translator
│   │   ├── pi-to-openai.ts       Response/SSE translator (multi-block merge, single chatcmpl-id, usage map, finish_reason map, mid-stream error frame)
│   │   └── model-id.ts           provider/model-id parser
│   └── server/
│       ├── index.ts              Programmatic API (startServer/stopServer) for tests + embedding
│       └── http-server.ts        node:http + inline router/auth/CORS/logger + SSE heartbeat
├── schemas/
│   └── openresponses.openapi.json   Pinned OpenAPI doc
├── tests/
│   ├── smoke.mjs                 Registration + bind + /healthz + /v1/models
│   ├── extension-e2e.mjs         Real HTTP against bound server; error paths
│   └── contract.mjs              ajv field-by-field validation
├── scripts/
│   ├── setup-git-hooks.mjs       prepare hook
│   └── build-check.mjs           emit dist/cli.js + shebang + chmod
├── .github/workflows/
│   ├── ci.yml                    pnpm run check on push + PR
│   └── publish.yml               Canary on main push, release on workflow_dispatch
├── .githooks/pre-commit          pnpm run check before commit
├── CLAUDE.md                     Project rules (this lives next to README.md)
├── README.md                     User-facing docs
├── llms.txt                      Short AI-consumption hub
├── llms-full.txt                 This file
├── AGENTS.md                     Pointer to pi-mono docs + repo conventions
└── LICENSE                       MIT
```

## Architectural patterns

### Hybrid package shape

| Surface | Entry | Loaded via |
|---------|-------|-----------|
| Standalone CLI | `dist/cli.js` (built from `src/cli.ts`) | `bin: pi-gateway` field |
| Pi extension | `index.ts` | `pi.extensions: ["./index.ts"]`; jiti-loaded inside pi runtime |
| Programmatic SDK | `dist/server/index.js` | Default `exports`/`main`/`types` |

The pi extension spawns the standalone binary as a **detached child** via `process.execPath` + `child.unref()` (mirrors `pi-claude-code/index.ts:187 launchDetachedRunner`). Binary resolution: prefer `dist/cli.js`; fall back to `src/cli.ts` via `@mariozechner/jiti` when `dist/` is absent (pi's auto-install via `git:` URL does NOT build).

### Dependencies (NOT peer)

`@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent`, `@earendil-works/pi-ai`, `@sinclair/typebox` are **runtime dependencies** (not peerDeps) because the standalone CLI needs them at process start. Siblings (pi-claude-code, pi-git-worktrees) use peerDeps because they have no bin — pi-gateway differs by design.

### Programmatic API separate from CLI

`src/server/index.ts` exports `startServer({...})` / `stopServer(handle)`:

```ts
import { startServer, stopServer } from "pi-gateway";
import { DEFAULT_CONFIG } from "pi-gateway/config";  // future

const handle = await startServer({
  config: { ...DEFAULT_CONFIG, port: 0, bindAddress: "127.0.0.1" },
});

// ... use http://${handle.address.address}:${handle.address.port}

await stopServer(handle);
```

Tests use this to bind a real listener on `127.0.0.1:0` without invoking the self-executing `dist/cli.js`.

## Test gates (BLOCKING, run via `pnpm run check`)

1. **Lint** — `pnpm run lint` (eslint, `@vllnt/eslint-config/nodejs`)
2. **Typecheck** — `pnpm run typecheck` (`tsc --noEmit`)
3. **Build** — `pnpm run build` (emit `dist/cli.js` + shebang via `tsconfig.build.json`)
4. **Test** — three suites:
   - `tests/smoke.mjs` — jiti-load index.ts with mock ExtensionAPI; bind 127.0.0.1:0; check /healthz + /v1/models
   - `tests/extension-e2e.mjs` — real HTTP against bound server; error paths
   - `tests/contract.mjs` — ajv field-by-field validation against pinned OpenAPI + locally-pinned Chat Completions schemas

All four gates run on `pre-commit` via `simple-git-hooks` and on CI via `.github/workflows/ci.yml`.

## CI/CD

| Workflow | Trigger | Action |
|----------|---------|--------|
| `ci.yml` | push, PR | `pnpm run check` |
| `publish.yml` | push to main (paths affecting package) | Quality gates → publish canary `<version>-canary.<sha>` |
| `publish.yml` | `workflow_dispatch` | Quality gates → version-bump guard → conventional-commit changelog → tag `v<version>` → publish `latest` → GH Release |

Both publish jobs use `--provenance --access public` (requires the repo to be public on GitHub).

## Wiring clients

### Open WebUI

Settings → Connections → OpenAI API:
- Base URL: `http://127.0.0.1:4000/v1`
- API key: `pi-gateway` (any non-empty string on loopback)

### LibreChat

```yaml
endpoints:
  custom:
    - name: "pi-gateway"
      baseURL: "http://127.0.0.1:4000/v1"
      apiKey: "pi-gateway"
      models:
        fetch: true
```

### Cursor

Settings → Models → Override OpenAI Base URL → `http://127.0.0.1:4000/v1`. Cursor may need a bare model alias (it can be strict about slashes); add aliases under Settings → Models → Add Model mapping e.g. `claude-sonnet-4-5` → `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5`.

### Continue.dev

```json
{
  "models": [{
    "provider": "openai",
    "apiBase": "http://127.0.0.1:4000/v1",
    "apiKey": "pi-gateway",
    "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5",
    "title": "Claude (via pi-gateway)"
  }]
}
```

### Cline

VS Code settings → Cline → API Provider → OpenAI Compatible. Base URL `http://127.0.0.1:4000/v1`. Model id `provider/model-id`.

## Troubleshooting

- **Pi auto-clone fails for the private repo.** The `git:git@github.com:...` form is SSH; verify `ssh -T git@github.com` returns your username. If not, add an SSH key at github.com/settings/keys.
- **`gh repo create` fails.** Confirm `gh auth status` lists `repo` scope. If not: `gh auth refresh -s repo`.
- **Cursor says "model not found".** Cursor's URL builder may strip the slash; add an alias mapping in Cursor settings.
- **Open WebUI shows truncated reply with no error.** Likely an SSE buffering issue behind nginx. pi-gateway sets `X-Accel-Buffering: no` + `Cache-Control: no-cache, no-transform`; verify the proxy is honoring them.
- **Codex returns "Instructions are required".** Codex's Responses API requires a system message; the client must include one. pi-gateway will return HTTP 502 with the upstream error verbatim.

## Repo structure of related pi packages (for context)

- `bntvllnt/pi-claude-code` — Pi extension that delegates background coding tasks to Claude Code CLI via detached child processes. Pattern reference for spawn/PID/lifecycle.
- `bntvllnt/pi-git-worktrees` — Pi extension for safe git worktree management. Pattern reference for slash commands + footer widget + project trust.
- `bntvllnt/pi-gateway` — this package; LiteLLM-shape OpenAI-compat HTTP frontend.
